pluginDescription: Built-in FT8/FT4 standard QSO automation strategy
strategyOverviewDesc: This strategy plugin drives the standard FT8/FT4 QSO flow, including answering others' CQ, automatic target picking, timeout handling, and retry behavior.
autoReplyToCQDesc: While listening, automatically choose and call eligible stations calling CQ. This setting does not mean starting your own CQ.
autoReplyToDirectCallWhenStoppedDesc: When the operator is stopped and idle in TX6, a direct call to this callsign can wake transmit and be answered, while still respecting worked-station and conflict checks.
autoResumeCQAfterFailDesc: After several no-response rounds or the call-attempt limit, return to TX6/CQ and keep listening/calling; turn it off to stop and wait for manual handling.
autoResumeCQAfterSuccessDesc: After sending 73, return to TX6/CQ automatically. Turn it off if you want to review the log or retune after each QSO.
replyToWorkedStationsDesc: When enabled, direct callers are answered even if their callsign is already in your log. CQ callers that are already worked are still skipped.
distinguishWorkedStationsByBandDesc: On: only QSOs on the current band count as worked. Off: a callsign worked on any band counts as worked.
skipTx1Desc: When calling a CQ station, skip the TX1 grid exchange and send the signal report first. This saves one cycle, but the other station may not see your grid.
targetSelectionPriorityModeDesc: When automatically picking a CQ or direct-call target, choose whether to prefer new DXCC entities, new grids, or new callsigns first.
maxQSOTimeoutCyclesDesc: How many consecutive no-progress cycles in the current QSO step count as a timeout. In FT8/FT4, one cycle is roughly one transmit/receive period.
maxCallAttemptsDesc: While in the TX1 initial call step, how many cycles to repeat the call before treating the attempt as failed.
